goal: Add a side-effect-free live AICOS connector adapter that accepts a supplied AICOS card payload, requires a ready BPK-015 fetch/cache contract, and routes snapshots through BPK-010 intake. Do not perform network fetches, cache writes, provider calls, database writes, GitHub writes, route changes, package changes, deployment changes, or frontend changes.
scope_out: Do not perform live AICOS fetches or add network code.
Do not write cache, DB, GitHub, runtime, route, workflow, package, deployment, or frontend changes.
Do not change BPK-010 or BPK-015 behavior.
claims: A supplied AICOS payload can be routed through intake only when fetch/cache readiness is ready.
Malformed payloads are blocked before intake.
Accepted and quarantined card counts are surfaced deterministically.
The connector adapter is side-effect-free and tested independently.
assumptions: A later block may perform the actual HTTP fetch and supply this payload.
BPK-010 remains the only path from raw card snapshots to dispatch-compatible cards.
BPK-015 remains the readiness gate for live fetch/cache policy.
dod: builder/src/liveAicosConnectorThroughIntake.ts exports side-effect-free connector adapter types and function.
The adapter requires a ready BPK-015 contract before accepting payloads.
The adapter validates payload shape and routes snapshots through BPK-010 intake.
builder/tests/liveAicosConnectorThroughIntake.test.ts covers ready payload, blocked contract, review contract, malformed payload, intake quarantine, and no-mutation behavior.
review-packets/BPK-019.md records scope, safety invariants, gates, and verification.
The focused connector-through-intake test and npm run typecheck in builder/ pass.
stop_conditions: A live fetch, cache write, route, provider call, DB write, GitHub write, package change, deployment change, workflow change, or frontend file becomes necessary.
Changing BPK-010 or BPK-015 behavior becomes necessary.
prior_task_findings: BPK-010 created intake quarantine and BPK-015 created fetch/cache readiness. BPK-019 should connect supplied payloads through those contracts without network or persistence side effects.
